km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Simon Combe是Richard Combe的兒子，於1275年出生在英國。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Simon Combe是Richard Combe的儿子，于1275年出生在英国。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Combe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Michel.
Scottish (Midlothian and East Lothian): from Gaelic Mac Thom ‘son of Thom’, see Thomas . It is also a shortened form of McCombe .

variants.name: Thomas
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Comeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Camille, Alphie, Emile, Fernand, Herve, Jacques, Marcel, Monique, Murielle, Normand.
topographic name from a diminutive of come, a variant of combe ‘narrow valley, ravine’ (see Combe ).

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Simon Combe was born in 1275, in England as the son of Richard Combe. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Simon Combe est né en 1275, à Angleterre, fils de Richard Combe. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Simon Combe wurde 1275 in England, als Sohn von Richard Combe geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Simon Combe è nato il nel 1275 nel England come figlio di Richard Combe. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Simon Combeは1275年にイギリスでRichard Combeの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Simon Combe(은)는 1275년 영국에서 Richard Combe의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Simon Combe nasceu em 1275, em Inglaterra filho de Richard Combe. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Simon Combe родился в 1275 г. в местности Англия. Его отца звали Richard Combe. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Simon Combe nació en 1275, en Inglaterra como hijo de Richard Combe. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
